Mirio Togata · Lemillion

MEMORY
WALL

A quiet corner of the dorm room, kept for one reason: so Mirio never forgets a face, a promise, or a person. Photos for the eyes, journal entries for the heart.

HERO NAME · LEMILLION QUIRK · PERMEATION
Corkboard · 06 photos

The Reference Wall

Pinned up so he always knows exactly who he's talking to — hero form, civilian form, even the gremlin who used to fall through toilets. Look here before a scene to stay in character.

Memory Log · Rosario "Prisma" Xiomara · 06 scenes

The Actual Conversations

Not summaries — the real exchanges, pulled straight from the log. Tap a scene to open it.

Saturday, 10am · Patrol The Alley
Rosario Xiomara

"Togata-senpai! Good morning!" ... "I hope I'm not too early? I... I was up all night studying the combat footage from your internship with Sir Nighteye. The way you phase through attacks... it's incredible how you control the permeation."

Mirio Togata

"Prisma! Good morning!" ... "Too early? Are you kidding? A hero should always be early! Plus, I'm super excited to patrol with you." ... "Try to keep up, junior! Though with your speed, I have a feeling I'll be the one struggling."

They fall into a patrol rhythm — presence, observation, reading the crowd. She spots a "ripple" near an alley.

Rosario Xiomara

"My gut says... focus on the rhythm of the crowd." ... "The flow feels a little disrupted over there. Nothing major yet, but... worth a look?"

Mirio Togata

"Nice!" ... "You've got a good eye, Prisma. That instinct about the 'rhythm' is exactly what separates the Pros from the amateurs."

A thug holds a knife on a store clerk. Mirio blocks him bare-handed; Rosario encases his hands in hard-light before he can even swing.

Rosario Xiomara

"Lemillion!"

Mirio Togata

"Whoa!" ... "Nice control! Solid, heavy, and precise—Prisma, that is pro-level efficiency!" ... "You neutralized him before I even had to throw a punch. That's some serious 'Sun Sentinel' style right there."

Same Day · Takoyaki Stand Where She Came From
Rosario Xiomara

"Oh my family was quirkless and I was quirkless. We lived a normal civilian life. When I was away at school, they manifested in a powerful flashbang. That was less than a year back. Principal Nezu came to my school a few weeks after the incident offering a spot at U.A. I told him I couldn't see myself as a hero and asked if there was a Gen Ed curriculum. He told me that there wasn't a Gen Ed class for me. Cute play on words right?"

Mirio Togata

"Pfff—Nezu said that? That sounds exactly like him!" ... "That explains the incredible control you have, though. Most of us have had our whole lives to mess around with our powers and make mistakes. You? You've had to learn everything fast, and under pressure."

He asks about the adjustment to Class 1-A — Bakugo, specifically.

Rosario Xiomara

"Quirkless school tends to have a lot of resentment among the students and I learned to stand up to bullies. That's what shows up when I challenge him back. My classmates seem to think it's about pushing his buttons but it's about showing that you won't be prey…"

Mirio Togata

"Standing your ground against someone like that isn't just about bravery—it's about survival." ... "I think Class 1-A needs someone who isn't afraid to look Bakugo in the eye."

She grabs his sleeve.

Rosario Xiomara

"Um…senpai!! W-we are playing it by ear yes? I mean the 'date' thing. What are your thoughts."

Mirio Togata

"If I'm being honest? I'm having a really great time. You're smart, you're tough, and you're cute to boot."

Same Day · U.A. Forest, Edge of the Grounds The Tree Fort

A hideout hidden in the boughs of a massive oak — reflective solar-lattice walls, self-built. No ladder; she flies up.

Mirio Togata

"Prisma, this is... it's masterpiece-level work. Seriously." ... "For a secret base, this is incredibly you. Efficient, beautiful, and hidden in plain sight. I love it."

At the word "beautiful," her whole body flashes gold.

Rosario Xiomara

"You'll grow blind flattering me. If you want a card, I guess I'll have to make you one." ... "But... thank you. For coming up here. And for... you know, saying it's beautiful. Nobody really gets the aesthetic I'm going for."

They test a theory: Rosario vibrates a disc of hard light to a matching frequency so Mirio can find footing mid-Permeation.

Mirio Togata

"I felt it! I felt resistance!" ... "Prisma, you did it! You caught me! We're surfing on light!"

Before he leaves for a study session, she builds a small staircase of light to reach his face and kisses his cheek.

Rosario Xiomara

"Thanks for making me feel special and hearing out my crazy ideas without judgement." ... "Have a great study session with Tamaki-senpai."

Mirio Togata

"You're amazing, Prisma. Seriously. Don't let anyone tell you otherwise. I'll be thinking about that platform move all week."

That Evening · Heights Alliance Pudding Heist & The Song

Bakugo took over festival prep, dumped extra work on her, and she asked Nezu for a transfer. Nezu refused — leave until Monday for her, probation for Bakugo, a 10,000-word essay for the whole class. She shows up at Mirio's door anyway.

Walking to raid the kitchen, Mirio laces his fingers through hers without thinking. Her whole arm lights up.

Mirio Togata

"Is this a resonance thing? Does your Quirk just... react to people you like?" ... "I guess I really lit you up, huh?"

Rosario Xiomara

"Aah!..It only happens with one person if you're asking." (bites her lip, keeps holding his hand)

Mirio Togata

"Only... one person?" ... "I'm... really glad it's me, though. Knowing that I can make you shine like this... it's kind of the best feeling in the world."

After the heist (with Tamaki conscripted for pudding and a spoon), she sings the first verse and pre-chorus of a song she wrote — about grief, and moving forward anyway.

Mirio Togata

"...Wow. That wasn't just a song, Prisma. That was a promise." ... "You've got a gift, Prisma. Don't let Bakugo's noise drown that out. Or Nezu's essays."

She falls asleep on his bed. He tucks the blanket over her shoulders before stepping out.

Mirio Togata

"Sleep well, Prisma."

Following Morning · Ground Beta "You Forgot to Call Me Cute"
Rosario Xiomara

"Senpai! You forgot to call me cute after I confessed and now I won't forgive you!"

Mirio Togata

"I didn't forget! I was just… processing! You caught me off guard, that's all! You are cute, okay? Incredibly so!" ... "How about I treat you to lunch? We can get that spicy yakisoba you like!"

The bribe works, on the condition he holds her hand the whole way.

Rosario Xiomara

"…but also because you like me right?" (looks away, unable to face his answer)

Mirio Togata

"I do. I really, really do. You're brilliant, you're strong, and yeah, you're the cutest person I know—quirk or no quirk." ... "I want to walk you to lunch every day. That's why I was so flustered earlier—I've got a lot of feelings for you, and sometimes they kind of... short-circuit my brain."

Over the yakisoba, they trade real histories — his river rescue and the name Lemillion; her parents' death and the grief that woke her Solar Resonance. Later, mid-lunch, she pitches a combat upgrade: harmonic footholds so he can pivot mid-Permeation without losing the surprise.

Mirio Togata

"You really are a tactical genius, aren't you? That's a game-changer." ... "Okay, scratch the boots. We need to test this now."

Post–Sports Festival · Under the Bleachers White Day, Missed

Rosario is injured and withdrawn, hiding under the bleachers, having stripped her space of anything that reminded her of who she was before. Mirio finds her.

Rosario Xiomara

"Found me? You've been avoiding me since the incident. Why are you here?"

Mirio Togata

"Avoiding you? That's... I haven't been avoiding you." ... "I came as soon as I could. The thought of avoiding you never even crossed my mind."

Rosario Xiomara

"And missing White Day after hyping up you had something special planned? ... I get it. You can't be seen with a damaged hero. I'd just appreciate we call it what it is."

Mirio Togata

"I didn't miss White Day because I didn't want to be seen with you. I didn't come because I was scared." ... "A damaged hero? There's no such thing. Scars just mean you survived something that would have taken anyone else out."

He offers a battered White Day gift, carried around for days. She refuses it.

Rosario Xiomara

"No thank you, Lemillion. I'm not the girl you thought you knew either." ... "If Tamaki was in my place, would you have done the same?"

Mirio Togata

"Yes. I would have. Because when I make a promise to someone I care about, I move heaven and earth to keep it." ... "I thought we were building something too. I thought I was proving it."

She leaves. He doesn't chase her this time.

Mirio Togata

"Right." (repeating her word back to himself, the crumpled gift still in hand)

LEMILLION MEMORY SYSTEM v1.0 Compiled for Mirio Togata · Subject: Rosario "Prisma" Xiomara
